[[画像:20070204宇都宮丘陵Tagged.jpg|thumb|right|300px|右手宇都宮丘陵。]] '''宇都宮丘陵'''（うつのみやきゅうりょう）とは[[栃木県]][[宇都宮市]]にあり市街地中心部のから北へ伸びる[[丘陵]]である。
==概要== 宇都宮丘陵は[[明神山 (宇都宮市)|明神山]]の[[宇都宮二荒山神社]]を南端として、[[八幡山 (宇都宮市)|八幡山]]、[[戸祭山]]へと北へ伸びる。
==土地利用== 市街地に立地しているため神社、公園、競輪場、住宅団地、学校、霊園等土地利用が進む。その他に北部には[[長岡百穴古墳]]などの古墳群がある。

revision.text:
[[画像:20070204宇都宮丘陵Tagged.jpg|thumb|right|300px|右手宇都宮丘陵。]] '''宇都宮丘陵'''（うつのみやきゅうりょう）とは[[栃木県]][[宇都宮市]]にあり市街地中心部から北へ伸びる[[丘陵]]である。
==概要== 宇都宮丘陵は[[明神山 (宇都宮市)|明神山]]の[[宇都宮二荒山神社]]を南端として、[[八幡山 (宇都宮市)|八幡山]]、[[戸祭山]]へと北へ伸びる。
==土地利用== [[市街地]]に立地しているため神社、公園、競輪場、住宅団地、学校、霊園等土地利用が進む。その他に北部には[[長岡百穴古墳]]などの古墳群がある。
<!-- ==交通==
==丘陵内に立地する主な施設== ===主な温泉===
===公園・運動場===
===キャンプ場===
===その他===
==立地するゴルフ場== --> ==関連項目== *[[羽黒山 (栃木県)|羽黒山]]
==外部リンク== *[http://maps.google.com/maps?ll=36.611792,139.835127&spn=.215590,.200260&t=k&hl=ja 宇都宮丘陵マップ]
{{DEFAULTSORT:うつのみやきゆうりよう}} [[Category:日本の丘陵]] [[Category:栃木県の地形]] [[Category:宇都宮市の地理]]

[[画像:20070204宇都宮丘陵Tagged.jpg|thumb|right|300px|右手が宇都宮丘陵。]] '''宇都宮丘陵'''（うつのみやきゅうりょう）とは[[栃木県]][[宇都宮市]]にあり市街地中心部から北へ伸びる[[丘陵]]である。
== 概要 == [[帝釈山脈]]や[[足尾山地]]から[[関東平野]]に向けて樹枝状に伸びる丘陵の1つである{{sfn|澁谷・梁木・清地|2019|p=2}}。市街地側から見ると、[[明神山 (宇都宮市)|明神山]]の[[宇都宮二荒山神社]]を南端として、[[八幡山 (宇都宮市)|八幡山]]、[[戸祭山]]へと北へ伸びている。丘陵の東西の裾野を[[田川 (利根川水系)|田川]]や[[釜川]]が流れる。
丘陵を構成する基盤は[[凝灰岩]]であり、広義の[[大谷石]]の一種である長岡石が採掘されている{{sfn|澁谷・梁木・清地|2019|p=2}}。表層部は[[ローム (土壌)|ローム]]や[[軽石]]などの[[火山噴出物]]に覆われる{{sfn|澁谷・梁木・清地|2019|p=2}}。
== 土地利用 == [[市街地]]に立地しているため[[神社]]、[[公園]]、[[宇都宮競輪場|競輪場]]、住宅団地、[[学校]]、[[霊園]]等土地開発が進む。[[旧石器時代]]以降の各時代の遺跡が集中する地域でもあり、[[戸祭大塚古墳]]、[[谷口山古墳]]、[[長岡百穴古墳]]などの[[古墳群]]がある{{sfn|澁谷・梁木・清地|2019|pp=2-4}}。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|author=澁谷麻友子・梁木誠・清地良太|title=瓦塚古墳|date=2019-03-29|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/71238|ncid=BB28934995|doi=10.24484/sitereports.71238|series=宇都宮市埋蔵文化財調査報告書104|publisher=宇都宮市教育委員会|editor=宇都宮市教育委員会 編|ref={{sfnref|澁谷・梁木・清地|2019}}}}
== 関連項目 == * [[羽黒山 (栃木県)]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://maps.google.com/maps?ll=36.611792,139.835127&spn=.215590,.200260&t=k&hl=ja 宇都宮丘陵マップ]
{{DEFAULTSORT:うつのみやきゆうりよう}} [[Category:日本の丘陵]] [[Category:栃木県の地形]] [[Category:宇都宮市の地理]]

revision.comment: /* 土地利用 */ [[瓦塚古墳群]]追加。

revision.text:
[[画像:20070204宇都宮丘陵Tagged.jpg|thumb|right|300px|右手が宇都宮丘陵。]] '''宇都宮丘陵'''（うつのみやきゅうりょう）とは[[栃木県]][[宇都宮市]]にあり市街地中心部から北へ伸びる[[丘陵]]である。
== 概要 == [[帝釈山脈]]や[[足尾山地]]から[[関東平野]]に向けて樹枝状に伸びる丘陵の1つである{{sfn|澁谷・梁木・清地|2019|p=2}}。市街地側から見ると、[[明神山 (宇都宮市)|明神山]]の[[宇都宮二荒山神社]]を南端として、[[八幡山 (宇都宮市)|八幡山]]、[[戸祭山]]へと北へ伸びている。丘陵の東西の裾野を[[田川 (利根川水系)|田川]]や[[釜川]]が流れる。
丘陵を構成する基盤は[[凝灰岩]]であり、広義の[[大谷石]]の一種である長岡石が採掘されている{{sfn|澁谷・梁木・清地|2019|p=2}}。表層部は[[ローム (土壌)|ローム]]や[[軽石]]などの[[火山噴出物]]に覆われる{{sfn|澁谷・梁木・清地|2019|p=2}}。
== 土地利用 == [[市街地]]に立地しているため[[神社]]、[[公園]]、[[宇都宮競輪場|競輪場]]、住宅団地、[[学校]]、[[霊園]]等土地開発が進む。[[旧石器時代]]以降の各時代の遺跡が集中する地域でもあり、[[戸祭大塚古墳]]、[[谷口山古墳]]、[[長岡百穴古墳]]、[[瓦塚古墳群]]などの[[古墳群]]がある{{sfn|澁谷・梁木・清地|2019|pp=2-4}}。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|author=澁谷麻友子・梁木誠・清地良太|title=瓦塚古墳|date=2019-03-29|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/71238|ncid=BB28934995|doi=10.24484/sitereports.71238|series=宇都宮市埋蔵文化財調査報告書104|publisher=宇都宮市教育委員会|editor=宇都宮市教育委員会|ref={{sfnref|澁谷・梁木・清地|2019}}}}
== 関連項目 == * [[羽黒山 (栃木県)]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://maps.google.com/maps?ll=36.611792,139.835127&spn=.215590,.200260&t=k&hl=ja 宇都宮丘陵マップ]
{{DEFAULTSORT:うつのみやきゆうりよう}} [[Category:日本の丘陵]] [[Category:栃木県の地形]] [[Category:宇都宮市の地理]] [[Category:うつのみや百景]]

[[ja:%E5%AE%87%E9%83%BD%E5%AE%AE%E4%B8%98%E9%99%B5]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Utsunomiya Hills}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Utsunomiya Hills}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]]

[[File:20070204宇都宮丘陵Tagged.jpg|thumb|right|300px|The right side shows the Utsunomiya Hills.]] '''Utsunomiya Hills''' (宇都宮丘陵) refers to a series of [[hills]] located in [[Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]], stretching northward from the central city area.
== Overview == The Utsunomiya Hills are one of the branching ridges extending in a dendritic pattern from the [[Taishaku Mountain Range]] and the [[Ashio Mountains]] toward the [[Kantō Plain]].<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019">{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=2}}</ref> Viewed from the city, the hills begin at [[Myōjin-yama (Utsunomiya)|Myōjin-yama]], near the {{ill|Utsunomiya Futarasan Shrine|ja|宇都宮二荒山神社}}, and extend northward through [[Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|Hachiman-yama]] to [[Tomatsuri-yama]]. The foothills are flanked by the [[Tagawa River (Tonegawa Basin)|Tagawa]] to the east and the [[Kamagawa River]] to the west.
The bedrock of the hills consists of [[tuff]], a type of stone quarried as Nagaoka Stone, which is considered a variant of {{ill|Ōya Stone|ja|大谷石}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019"/> The surface is covered by layers of [[loam]], [[pumice]], and other [[volcanic ejecta]].<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019"/>
== Land Use == Due to its location within the city, the Utsunomiya Hills have been developed for various uses, including {{ill|shrines|ja|神社}}, {{ill|parks|ja|公園}}, the {{ill|Utsunomiya Velodrome|ja|宇都宮競輪場}}, residential areas, {{ill|schools|ja|学校}}, and cemeteries. It is also an area of historical significance, with numerous archaeological sites from the {{ill|Paleolithic period in Japan|ja|旧石器時代}} onwards. The region contains several {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} clusters, including the [[Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]], [[Yaguchiyama Kofun]], [[Nagaoka Hyakka Kofun]], and [[Kawazuka Kofun Group]].<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019">{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|pp=2-4}}</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|author=Shibuya Mayuko, Yanagi Makoto, Seichi Ryota|title=Kawazuka Kofun|date=2019-03-29|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/71238|ncid=BB28934995|doi=10.24484/sitereports.71238|series=Utsunomiya City Archaeological Survey Report No. 104|publisher=Utsunomiya City Board of Education|editor=Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ref={{sfnref|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019}}}}
== Related Topics == * [[Haguroyama (Tochigi)]]
== External Links == * [https://www.google.com/maps?ll=36.611792,139.835127&spn=.215590,.200260&t=k&hl=ja Utsunomiya Hills Map]

[[File:20070204宇都宮丘陵Tagged.jpg|thumb|right|300px|The right side shows the Utsunomiya Hills.]] '''Utsunomiya Hills''' (宇都宮丘陵) refers to a series of {{ill|hills|ja|丘陵}} located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, stretching northward from the central city area.
== Overview == The Utsunomiya Hills are one of the branching ridges extending in a dendritic pattern from the {{ill|Taishaku Mountain Range|ja|帝釈山脈}} and the {{ill|Ashio Mountains|ja|足尾山地}} toward the {{ill|Kantō Plain|ja|関東平野}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019">{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=2}}</ref> Viewed from the city, the hills begin at {{ill|Myōjin-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|明神山}}, near the [[Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine]], and extend northward through {{ill|Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|八幡山}} to {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山}}. The foothills are flanked by the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tonegawa Basin)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}} to the east and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川}} to the west.
The bedrock of the hills consists of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}, a type of stone quarried as Nagaoka Stone, which is considered a variant of {{ill|Ōya Stone|ja|大谷石}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019"/> The surface is covered by layers of {{ill|loam|ja|ローム (土壌)}}, {{ill|pumice|ja|軽石}}, and other {{ill|ejecta|ja|火山噴出物|volcanic ejecta=volcanic ejecta|lt=volcanic ejecta}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019"/>
== Land Use == Due to its location within the {{ill|city area|ja|市街地}}, the Utsunomiya Hills have been developed for various purposes, including {{ill|shrines|ja|神社}}, {{ill|parks|ja|公園}}, the {{ill|Utsunomiya Velodrome|ja|宇都宮競輪場}}, residential areas, {{ill|schools|ja|学校}}, and cemeteries. It is also an area of historical significance, with numerous archaeological sites from the {{ill|Paleolithic period in Japan|ja|旧石器時代}} onwards. The region contains several {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} clusters, including the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakka Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳}}, and {{ill|Kawazuka Kofun Group|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019">{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|pp=2-4}}</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|author=Shibuya Mayuko, Yanagi Makoto, Seichi Ryota|title=Kawazuka Kofun|date=2019-03-29|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/71238|ncid=BB28934995|doi=10.24484/sitereports.71238|series=Utsunomiya City Archaeological Survey Report No. 104|publisher=Utsunomiya City Board of Education|editor=Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ref={{sfnref|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019}}}}

[[File:20070204宇都宮丘陵Tagged.jpg|thumb|right|300px|The right side shows the Utsunomiya Hills.]] '''Utsunomiya Hills''' (宇都宮丘陵) refers to a series of {{ill|hills|ja|丘陵}} located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, stretching northward from the central city area.
== Overview == The Utsunomiya Hills are one of the branching ridges extending in a dendritic pattern from the {{ill|Taishaku Mountain Range|ja|帝釈山脈}} and the {{ill|Ashio Mountains|ja|足尾山地}} toward the {{ill|Kantō Plain|ja|関東平野}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019">{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=2}}</ref> Viewed from the city, the hills begin at {{ill|Myōjin-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|明神山}}, near the [[Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine]], and extend northward through {{ill|Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|八幡山}} to {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山}}. The foothills are flanked by the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tonegawa Basin)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}} to the east and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川}} to the west.
The bedrock of the hills consists of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}, a type of stone quarried as Nagaoka Stone, which is considered a variant of {{ill|Ōya Stone|ja|大谷石}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019"/> The surface is covered by layers of {{ill|loam|ja|ローム (土壌)}}, {{ill|pumice|ja|軽石}}, and other {{ill|ejecta|ja|火山噴出物|volcanic ejecta=volcanic ejecta|lt=volcanic ejecta}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019"/>
== Land Use == Due to its location within the {{ill|city area|ja|市街地}}, the Utsunomiya Hills have been developed for various purposes, including {{ill|shrines|ja|神社}}, {{ill|parks|ja|公園}}, the {{ill|Utsunomiya Velodrome|ja|宇都宮競輪場}}, residential areas, {{ill|schools|ja|学校}}, and cemeteries. It is also an area of historical significance, with numerous archaeological sites from the {{ill|Paleolithic period in Japan|ja|旧石器時代}} onwards. The region contains several {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} clusters, including the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakka Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳}}, and {{ill|Kawazuka Kofun Group|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019">{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|pp=2-4}}</ref>

[[File:20070204宇都宮丘陵Tagged.jpg|thumb|right|300px|The right side shows the Utsunomiya Hills.]] '''Utsunomiya Hills''' (宇都宮丘陵) refers to a series of {{ill|hills|ja|丘陵}} located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, stretching northward from the central city area.
== Overview == The Utsunomiya Hills are one of the branching ridges extending in a dendritic pattern from the {{ill|Taishaku Mountain Range|ja|帝釈山脈}} and the {{ill|Ashio Mountains|ja|足尾山地}} toward the {{ill|Kantō Plain|ja|関東平野}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019">{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=2}}</ref> Viewed from the city, the hills begin at {{ill|Myōjin-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|明神山}}, near the [[Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine]], and extend northward through {{ill|Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|八幡山}} to {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山}}. The foothills are flanked by the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tonegawa Basin)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}} to the east and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川}} to the west.
The bedrock of the hills consists of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}, a type of stone quarried as Nagaoka Stone, which is considered a variant of {{ill|Ōya Stone|ja|大谷石}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019"/> The surface is covered by layers of {{ill|loam|ja|ローム (土壌)}}, {{ill|pumice|ja|軽石}}, and other {{ill|ejecta|ja|火山噴出物|volcanic ejecta=volcanic ejecta|lt=volcanic ejecta}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019"/>
== Land Use == Due to its location within the {{ill|city area|ja|市街地}}, the Utsunomiya Hills have been developed for various purposes, including {{ill|shrines|ja|神社}}, {{ill|parks|ja|公園}}, the {{ill|Utsunomiya Velodrome|ja|宇都宮競輪場}}, residential areas, {{ill|schools|ja|学校}}, and cemeteries. It is also an area of historical significance, with numerous archaeological sites from the {{ill|Paleolithic period in Japan|ja|旧石器時代}} onwards. The region contains several {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} clusters, including the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakka Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳}}, and {{ill|Kawazuka Kofun Group|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019">{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|pp=2-4}}</ref>

[[File:20070204宇都宮丘陵Tagged.jpg|thumb|right|300px|The right side shows the Utsunomiya Hills.]] '''Utsunomiya Hills''' (宇都宮丘陵) refers to a series of {{ill|hills|ja|丘陵}} located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, stretching northward from the central city area.
== Overview == The Utsunomiya Hills are one of the branching ridges extending in a dendritic pattern from the {{ill|Taishaku Mountain Range|ja|帝釈山脈}} and the {{ill|Ashio Mountains|ja|足尾山地}} toward the {{ill|Kantō Plain|ja|関東平野}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019">{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=2}}</ref> Viewed from the city, the hills begin at {{ill|Myōjin-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|明神山}}, near the [[Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine]], and extend northward through {{ill|Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|八幡山}} to {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山}}. The foothills are flanked by the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tonegawa Basin)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}} to the east and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川}} to the west.
The bedrock of the hills consists of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}, a type of stone quarried as Nagaoka Stone, which is considered a variant of {{ill|Ōya Stone|ja|大谷石}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019"/> The surface is covered by layers of {{ill|loam|ja|ローム (土壌)}}, {{ill|pumice|ja|軽石}}, and other {{ill|ejecta|ja|火山噴出物|volcanic ejecta=volcanic ejecta|lt=volcanic ejecta}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019"/>
== Land Use == Due to its location within the {{ill|city area|ja|市街地}}, the Utsunomiya Hills have been developed for various purposes, including {{ill|shrines|ja|神社}}, {{ill|parks|ja|公園}}, the {{ill|Utsunomiya Velodrome|ja|宇都宮競輪場}}, residential areas, {{ill|schools|ja|学校}}, and cemeteries. It is also an area of historical significance, with numerous archaeological sites from the {{ill|Paleolithic period in Japan|ja|旧石器時代}} onwards. The region contains several {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} clusters, including the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakka Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳}}, and {{ill|Kawazuka Kofun Group|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019">{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|pp=2-4}}</ref>

The region contains several {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} clusters, including the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakka Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳}}, and {{ill|Kawazuka Kofun Group|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019">{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|pp=2-4}}</ref>

[[File:20070204宇都宮丘陵Tagged.jpg|thumb|right|300px|The right side shows the Utsunomiya Hills.]] '''Utsunomiya Hills''' (宇都宮丘陵) refers to a series of {{ill|hills|ja|丘陵}} located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, stretching northward from the central city area.
== Overview == The Utsunomiya Hills are one of the branching ridges extending in a dendritic pattern from the {{ill|Taishaku Mountain Range|ja|帝釈山脈}} and the {{ill|Ashio Mountains|ja|足尾山地}} toward the {{ill|Kantō Plain|ja|関東平野}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019">{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=2}}</ref> Viewed from the city, the hills begin at {{ill|Myōjin-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|明神山}}, near the [[Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine]], and extend northward through {{ill|Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|八幡山}} to {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山}}. The foothills are flanked by the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tonegawa Basin)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}} to the east and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川}} to the west.
The bedrock of the hills consists of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}, a type of stone quarried as Nagaoka Stone, which is considered a variant of {{ill|Ōya Stone|ja|大谷石}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019"/> The surface is covered by layers of {{ill|loam|ja|ローム (土壌)}}, {{ill|pumice|ja|軽石}}, and other {{ill|ejecta|ja|火山噴出物|volcanic ejecta=volcanic ejecta|lt=volcanic ejecta}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019"/>
== Land Use == Due to its location within the {{ill|city area|ja|市街地}}, the Utsunomiya Hills have been developed for various purposes, including {{ill|shrines|ja|神社}}, {{ill|parks|ja|公園}}, the {{ill|Utsunomiya Velodrome|ja|宇都宮競輪場}}, residential areas, {{ill|schools|ja|学校}}, and cemeteries. It is also an area of historical significance, with numerous archaeological sites from the {{ill|Paleolithic period in Japan|ja|旧石器時代}} onwards. The region contains several {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} clusters, including the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakka Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳}}, and {{ill|Kawazuka Kofun Group|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019">{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|pp=2-4}}</ref>

[[File:20070204宇都宮丘陵Tagged.jpg|thumb|right|300px|The right side shows the Utsunomiya Hills.]] '''Utsunomiya Hills''' (宇都宮丘陵) refers to a series of {{ill|hills|ja|丘陵}} located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, stretching northward from the central city area.
== Overview == The Utsunomiya Hills are one of the branching ridges extending in a dendritic pattern from the {{ill|Taishaku Mountain Range|ja|帝釈山脈}} and the {{ill|Ashio Mountains|ja|足尾山地}} toward the {{ill|Kantō Plain|ja|関東平野}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019">{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=2}}</ref> Viewed from the city, the hills begin at {{ill|Myōjin-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|明神山}}, near the [[Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine]], and extend northward through {{ill|Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|八幡山}} to {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山}}. The foothills are flanked by the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tonegawa Basin)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}} to the east and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川}} to the west.
The bedrock of the hills consists of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}, a type of stone quarried as Nagaoka Stone, which is considered a variant of {{ill|Ōya Stone|ja|大谷石}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019"/> The surface is covered by layers of {{ill|loam|ja|ローム (土壌)}}, {{ill|pumice|ja|軽石}}, and other {{ill|ejecta|ja|火山噴出物|volcanic ejecta=volcanic ejecta|lt=volcanic ejecta}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019"/>
== Land Use == Due to its location within the {{ill|city area|ja|市街地}}, the Utsunomiya Hills have been developed for various purposes, including {{ill|shrines|ja|神社}}, {{ill|parks|ja|公園}}, the {{ill|Utsunomiya Velodrome|ja|宇都宮競輪場}}, residential areas, {{ill|schools|ja|学校}}, and cemeteries. It is also an area of historical significance, with numerous archaeological sites from the {{ill|Paleolithic period in Japan|ja|旧石器時代}} onwards. The region contains several {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} clusters, including the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakka Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳}}, and {{ill|Kawazuka Kofun Group|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019">{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|pp=2-4}}</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|author=Shibuya Mayuko, Yanagi Makoto, Seichi Ryota|title=Kawazuka Kofun|date=2019-03-29|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/71238|ncid=BB28934995|doi=10.24484/sitereports.71238|series=Utsunomiya City Archaeological Survey Report No. 104|publisher=Utsunomiya City Board of Education|editor=Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ref={{sfnref|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019}}}}

== External Links == * [https://www.google.com/maps?ll=36.611792,139.835127&spn=.215590,.200260&t=k&hl=ja Utsunomiya Hills Map]

[[File:20070204宇都宮丘陵Tagged.jpg|thumb|right|300px|The right side shows the Utsunomiya Hills.]] '''Utsunomiya Hills''' (宇都宮丘陵) refers to a series of {{ill|hills|ja|丘陵}} located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, stretching northward from the central city area.
== Overview == The Utsunomiya Hills are one of the branching ridges extending in a dendritic pattern from the {{ill|Taishaku Mountain Range|ja|帝釈山脈}} and the {{ill|Ashio Mountains|ja|足尾山地}} toward the {{ill|Kantō Plain|ja|関東平野}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019">{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=2}}</ref> Viewed from the city, the hills begin at {{ill|Myōjin-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|明神山}}, near the [[Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine]], and extend northward through {{ill|Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|八幡山}} to {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山}}. The foothills are flanked by the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tonegawa Basin)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}} to the east and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川}} to the west.
The bedrock of the hills consists of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}, a type of stone quarried as Nagaoka Stone, which is considered a variant of {{ill|Ōya Stone|ja|大谷石}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019"/> The surface is covered by layers of {{ill|loam|ja|ローム (土壌)}}, {{ill|pumice|ja|軽石}}, and other {{ill|ejecta|ja|火山噴出物|volcanic ejecta=volcanic ejecta|lt=volcanic ejecta}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019"/>
== Land Use == Due to its location within the {{ill|city area|ja|市街地}}, the Utsunomiya Hills have been developed for various purposes, including {{ill|shrines|ja|神社}}, {{ill|parks|ja|公園}}, the {{ill|Utsunomiya Velodrome|ja|宇都宮競輪場}}, residential areas, {{ill|schools|ja|学校}}, and cemeteries. It is also an area of historical significance, with numerous archaeological sites from the {{ill|Paleolithic period in Japan|ja|旧石器時代}} onwards. The region contains several {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} clusters, including the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakka Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳}}, and {{ill|Kawazuka Kofun Group|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019">{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|pp=2-4}}</ref>

The region contains several {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} clusters, including the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakka Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳}}, and {{ill|Kawazuka Kofun Group|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019">{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|pp=2-4}}</ref>

[[File:20070204宇都宮丘陵Tagged.jpg|thumb|right|300px|The right side shows the Utsunomiya Hills.]] '''Utsunomiya Hills''' (宇都宮丘陵) refers to a series of {{ill|hills|ja|丘陵}} located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, stretching northward from the central city area.
== Overview == The Utsunomiya Hills are one of the branching ridges extending in a dendritic pattern from the {{ill|Taishaku Mountain Range|ja|帝釈山脈}} and the {{ill|Ashio Mountains|ja|足尾山地}} toward the {{ill|Kantō Plain|ja|関東平野}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019">{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=2}}</ref> Viewed from the city, the hills begin at {{ill|Myōjin-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|明神山}}, near the [[Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine]], and extend northward through {{ill|Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|八幡山}} to {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山}}. The foothills are flanked by the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tonegawa Basin)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}} to the east and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川}} to the west.
The bedrock of the hills consists of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}, a type of stone quarried as Nagaoka Stone, which is considered a variant of {{ill|Ōya Stone|ja|大谷石}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019"/> The surface is covered by layers of {{ill|loam|ja|ローム (土壌)}}, {{ill|pumice|ja|軽石}}, and other {{ill|ejecta|ja|火山噴出物|volcanic ejecta=volcanic ejecta|lt=volcanic ejecta}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019"/>
== Land Use == Due to its location within the {{ill|city area|ja|市街地}}, the Utsunomiya Hills have been developed for various purposes, including {{ill|shrines|ja|神社}}, {{ill|parks|ja|公園}}, the {{ill|Utsunomiya Velodrome|ja|宇都宮競輪場}}, residential areas, {{ill|schools|ja|学校}}, and cemeteries. It is also an area of historical significance, with numerous archaeological sites from the {{ill|Paleolithic period in Japan|ja|旧石器時代}} onwards. The region contains several {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} clusters, including the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakka Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳}}, and {{ill|Kawazuka Kofun Group|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019">{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|pp=2-4}}</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|author=Shibuya Mayuko, Yanagi Makoto, Seichi Ryota|title=Kawazuka Kofun|date=2019-03-29|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/71238|ncid=BB28934995|doi=10.24484/sitereports.71238|series=Utsunomiya City Archaeological Survey Report No. 104|publisher=Utsunomiya City Board of Education|editor=Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ref={{sfnref|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019}}}}

revision.text:
[[File:20070204宇都宮丘陵Tagged.jpg|thumb|right|300px|The right side shows the Utsunomiya Hills.]] '''Utsunomiya Hills''' (宇都宮丘陵) refers to a series of {{ill|hills|ja|丘陵}} located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, stretching northward from the central city area.
== Overview == The Utsunomiya Hills are one of the branching ridges extending in a dendritic pattern from the {{ill|Taishaku Mountain Range|ja|帝釈山脈}} and the {{ill|Ashio Mountains|ja|足尾山地}} toward the {{ill|Kantō Plain|ja|関東平野}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019">{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=2}}</ref> Viewed from the city, the hills begin at {{ill|Myōjin-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|明神山}}, near the [[Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine]], and extend northward through {{ill|Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|八幡山}} to {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山}}. The foothills are flanked by the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tonegawa Basin)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}} to the east and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川}} to the west.
The bedrock of the hills consists of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}, a type of stone quarried as Nagaoka Stone, which is considered a variant of {{ill|Ōya Stone|ja|大谷石}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019"/> The surface is covered by layers of {{ill|loam|ja|ローム (土壌)}}, {{ill|pumice|ja|軽石}}, and other {{ill|ejecta|ja|火山噴出物|volcanic ejecta=volcanic ejecta|lt=volcanic ejecta}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019"/>
== Land Use == Due to its location within the {{ill|city area|ja|市街地}}, the Utsunomiya Hills have been developed for various purposes, including {{ill|shrines|ja|神社}}, {{ill|parks|ja|公園}}, the {{ill|Utsunomiya Velodrome|ja|宇都宮競輪場}}, residential areas, {{ill|schools|ja|学校}}, and cemeteries. It is also an area of historical significance, with numerous archaeological sites from the {{ill|Paleolithic period in Japan|ja|旧石器時代}} onwards. The region contains several {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} clusters, including the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakka Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳}}, and {{ill|Kawazuka Kofun Group|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019">{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|pp=2-4}}</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|author=Shibuya Mayuko, Yanagi Makoto, Seichi Ryota|title=Kawazuka Kofun|date=2019-03-29|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/71238|ncid=BB28934995|doi=10.24484/sitereports.71238|series=Utsunomiya City Archaeological Survey Report No. 104|publisher=Utsunomiya City Board of Education|editor=Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ref={{sfnref|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019}}}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Haguroyama (Tochigi)|ja|羽黒山 (栃木県)}}
== External Links == * [https://www.google.com/maps?ll=36.611792,139.835127&spn=.215590,.200260&t=k&hl=ja Utsunomiya Hills Map]

[[File:20070204宇都宮丘陵Tagged.jpg|thumb|right|300px|The right side shows the Utsunomiya Hills.]] '''Utsunomiya Hills''' (宇都宮丘陵) refers to a series of {{ill|hills|ja|丘陵}} located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, stretching northward from the central city area.
== Overview == The Utsunomiya Hills are one of the branching ridges extending in a dendritic pattern from the {{ill|Taishaku Mountain Range|ja|帝釈山脈}} and the {{ill|Ashio Mountains|ja|足尾山地}} toward the {{ill|Kantō Plain|ja|関東平野}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019">{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=2}}</ref> Viewed from the city, the hills begin at {{ill|Myōjin-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|明神山}}, near the [[Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine]], and extend northward through {{ill|Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|八幡山}} to {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山}}. The foothills are flanked by the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tonegawa Basin)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}} to the east and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川}} to the west.
The bedrock of the hills consists of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}, a type of stone quarried as Nagaoka Stone, which is considered a variant of {{ill|Ōya Stone|ja|大谷石}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019"/> The surface is covered by layers of {{ill|loam|ja|ローム (土壌)}}, {{ill|pumice|ja|軽石}}, and other {{ill|ejecta|ja|火山噴出物|volcanic ejecta=volcanic ejecta|lt=volcanic ejecta}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019"/>
== Land Use == Due to its location within the {{ill|city area|ja|市街地}}, the Utsunomiya Hills have been developed for various purposes, including {{ill|shrines|ja|神社}}, {{ill|parks|ja|公園}}, the {{ill|Utsunomiya Velodrome|ja|宇都宮競輪場}}, residential areas, {{ill|schools|ja|学校}}, and cemeteries. It is also an area of historical significance, with numerous archaeological sites from the {{ill|Paleolithic period in Japan|ja|旧石器時代}} onwards. The region contains several {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} clusters, including the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakka Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳}}, and {{ill|Kawazuka Kofun Group|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019">{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|pp=2-4}}</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|author=Shibuya Mayuko, Yanagi Makoto, Seichi Ryota|title=Kawazuka Kofun|date=2019-03-29|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/71238|ncid=BB28934995|doi=10.24484/sitereports.71238|series=Utsunomiya City Archaeological Survey Report No. 104|publisher=Utsunomiya City Board of Education|editor=Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ref={{sfnref|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019}}}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Haguroyama (Tochigi)|ja|羽黒山 (栃木県)}}
== External Links == * [https://www.google.com/maps?ll=36.611792,139.835127&spn=.215590,.200260&t=k&hl=ja Utsunomiya Hills Map]

revision.text:
[[File:20070204宇都宮丘陵Tagged.jpg|thumb|right|300px|The right side shows the Utsunomiya Hills.]] '''Utsunomiya Hills''' (宇都宮丘陵) refers to a series of {{ill|hills|ja|丘陵}} located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, stretching northward from the central city area.
== Overview == The Utsunomiya Hills are one of the branching ridges extending in a dendritic pattern from the {{ill|Taishaku Mountain Range|ja|帝釈山脈}} and the {{ill|Ashio Mountains|ja|足尾山地}} toward the {{ill|Kantō Plain|ja|関東平野}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019">{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=2}}</ref> Viewed from the city, the hills begin at {{ill|Myōjin-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|明神山}}, near the [[Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine]], and extend northward through {{ill|Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|八幡山}} to {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山}}. The foothills are flanked by the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tonegawa Basin)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}} to the east and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川}} to the west.
The bedrock of the hills consists of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}, a type of stone quarried as Nagaoka Stone, which is considered a variant of {{ill|Ōya Stone|ja|大谷石}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019"/> The surface is covered by layers of {{ill|loam|ja|ローム (土壌)}}, {{ill|pumice|ja|軽石}}, and other {{ill|ejecta|ja|火山噴出物|volcanic ejecta=volcanic ejecta|lt=volcanic ejecta}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019"/>
== Land Use == Due to its location within the {{ill|city area|ja|市街地}}, the Utsunomiya Hills have been developed for various purposes, including {{ill|shrines|ja|神社}}, {{ill|parks|ja|公園}}, the {{ill|Utsunomiya Velodrome|ja|宇都宮競輪場}}, residential areas, {{ill|schools|ja|学校}}, and cemeteries. It is also an area of historical significance, with numerous archaeological sites from the {{ill|Paleolithic period in Japan|ja|旧石器時代}} onwards. The region contains several {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} clusters, including the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakka Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳}}, and {{ill|Kawazuka Kofun Group|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019">{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|pp=2-4}}</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|author=Shibuya Mayuko, Yanagi Makoto, Seichi Ryota|title=Kawazuka Kofun|date=2019-03-29|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/71238|ncid=BB28934995|doi=10.24484/sitereports.71238|series=Utsunomiya City Archaeological Survey Report No. 104|publisher=Utsunomiya City Board of Education|editor=Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ref={{sfnref|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019}}}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Haguroyama (Tochigi)|ja|羽黒山 (栃木県)}}
== External Links == * [https://www.google.com/maps?ll=36.611792,139.835127&spn=.215590,.200260&t=k&hl=ja Utsunomiya Hills Map]

revision.text:
[[File:20070204宇都宮丘陵Tagged.jpg|thumb|right|300px|The right side shows the Utsunomiya Hills.]] '''Utsunomiya Hills''' (宇都宮丘陵) refers to a series of {{ill|hills|ja|丘陵}} located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, stretching northward from the central city area.
== Overview == The Utsunomiya Hills are one of the branching ridges extending in a dendritic pattern from the {{ill|Taishaku Mountain Range|ja|帝釈山脈}} and the {{ill|Ashio Mountains|ja|足尾山地}} toward the {{ill|Kantō Plain|ja|関東平野}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019">{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=2}}</ref> Viewed from the city, the hills begin at {{ill|Myōjin-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|明神山}}, near the [[Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine]], and extend northward through {{ill|Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|八幡山}} to {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山}}. The foothills are flanked by the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tonegawa Basin)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}} to the east and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川}} to the west.
The bedrock of the hills consists of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}, a type of stone quarried as Nagaoka Stone, which is considered a variant of {{ill|Ōya Stone|ja|大谷石}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019"/> The surface is covered by layers of {{ill|loam|ja|ローム (土壌)}}, {{ill|pumice|ja|軽石}}, and other {{ill|ejecta|ja|火山噴出物|volcanic ejecta=volcanic ejecta|lt=volcanic ejecta}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019"/>
== Land Use == Due to its location within the {{ill|city area|ja|市街地}}, the Utsunomiya Hills have been developed for various purposes, including {{ill|shrines|ja|神社}}, {{ill|parks|ja|公園}}, the {{ill|Utsunomiya Velodrome|ja|宇都宮競輪場}}, residential areas, {{ill|schools|ja|学校}}, and cemeteries. It is also an area of historical significance, with numerous archaeological sites from the {{ill|Paleolithic period in Japan|ja|旧石器時代}} onwards. The region contains several {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} clusters, including the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Nagaoka Hundred Caves Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves}}, and {{ill|Kawazuka Kofun Group|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019">{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|pp=2-4}}</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|author=Shibuya Mayuko, Yanagi Makoto, Seichi Ryota|title=Kawazuka Kofun|date=2019-03-29|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/71238|ncid=BB28934995|doi=10.24484/sitereports.71238|series=Utsunomiya City Archaeological Survey Report No. 104|publisher=Utsunomiya City Board of Education|editor=Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ref={{sfnref|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019}}}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Haguroyama (Tochigi)|ja|羽黒山 (栃木県)}}
== External Links == * [https://www.google.com/maps?ll=36.611792,139.835127&spn=.215590,.200260&t=k&hl=ja Utsunomiya Hills Map]

[[File:20070204宇都宮丘陵Tagged.jpg|thumb|right|300px|The right side shows the Utsunomiya Hills.]] '''Utsunomiya Hills''' (宇都宮丘陵) refers to a series of {{ill|hills|ja|丘陵}} located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, stretching northward from the central city area.
== Overview == The Utsunomiya Hills are one of the branching ridges extending in a dendritic pattern from the {{ill|Taishaku Mountain Range|ja|帝釈山脈}} and the {{ill|Ashio Mountains|ja|足尾山地}} toward the {{ill|Kantō Plain|ja|関東平野}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019">{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=2}}</ref> Viewed from the city, the hills begin at {{ill|Myōjin-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|明神山}}, near the [[Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine]], and extend northward through {{ill|Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|八幡山}} to {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山}}. The foothills are flanked by the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tonegawa Basin)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}} to the east and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川}} to the west.
The bedrock of the hills consists of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}, a type of stone quarried as Nagaoka Stone, which is considered a variant of {{ill|Ōya Stone|ja|大谷石}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019"/> The surface is covered by layers of {{ill|loam|ja|ローム (土壌)}}, {{ill|pumice|ja|軽石}}, and other {{ill|ejecta|ja|火山噴出物|volcanic ejecta=volcanic ejecta|lt=volcanic ejecta}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019"/>
== Land Use == Due to its location within the {{ill|city area|ja|市街地}}, the Utsunomiya Hills have been developed for various purposes, including {{ill|shrines|ja|神社}}, {{ill|parks|ja|公園}}, the {{ill|Utsunomiya Velodrome|ja|宇都宮競輪場}}, residential areas, {{ill|schools|ja|学校}}, and cemeteries. It is also an area of historical significance, with numerous archaeological sites from the {{ill|Paleolithic period in Japan|ja|旧石器時代}} onwards. The region contains several {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} clusters, including the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Nagaoka Hundred Caves Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves}}, and {{ill|Kawazuka Kofun Group|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019">{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|pp=2-4}}</ref>

[[File:20070204宇都宮丘陵Tagged.jpg|thumb|right|300px|The right side shows the Utsunomiya Hills.]] '''Utsunomiya Hills''' (宇都宮丘陵) refers to a series of {{ill|hills|ja|丘陵}} located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, stretching northward from the central city area.
== Overview == The Utsunomiya Hills are one of the branching ridges extending in a dendritic pattern from the {{ill|Taishaku Mountain Range|ja|帝釈山脈}} and the {{ill|Ashio Mountains|ja|足尾山地}} toward the {{ill|Kantō Plain|ja|関東平野}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019">{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=2}}</ref> Viewed from the city, the hills begin at {{ill|Myōjin-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|明神山}}, near the [[Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine]], and extend northward through {{ill|Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|八幡山}} to {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山}}. The foothills are flanked by the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tonegawa Basin)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}} to the east and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川}} to the west.
The bedrock of the hills consists of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}, a type of stone quarried as Nagaoka Stone, which is considered a variant of {{ill|Ōya Stone|ja|大谷石}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019"/> The surface is covered by layers of {{ill|loam|ja|ローム (土壌)}}, {{ill|pumice|ja|軽石}}, and other {{ill|ejecta|ja|火山噴出物|volcanic ejecta=volcanic ejecta|lt=volcanic ejecta}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019"/>
== Land Use == Due to its location within the {{ill|city area|ja|市街地}}, the Utsunomiya Hills have been developed for various purposes, including {{ill|shrines|ja|神社}}, {{ill|parks|ja|公園}}, the {{ill|Utsunomiya Velodrome|ja|宇都宮競輪場}}, residential areas, {{ill|schools|ja|学校}}, and cemeteries. It is also an area of historical significance, with numerous archaeological sites from the {{ill|Paleolithic period in Japan|ja|旧石器時代}} onwards. The region contains several {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} clusters, including the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Nagaoka Hundred Caves Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves}}, and {{ill|Kawazuka Kofun Group|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019">{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|pp=2-4}}</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|author=Shibuya Mayuko, Yanagi Makoto, Seichi Ryota|title=Kawazuka Kofun|date=2019-03-29|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/71238|ncid=BB28934995|doi=10.24484/sitereports.71238|series=Utsunomiya City Archaeological Survey Report No. 104|publisher=Utsunomiya City Board of Education|editor=Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ref={{sfnref|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019}}}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Haguroyama (Tochigi)|ja|羽黒山 (栃木県)}}
== External Links == * [https://www.google.com/maps?ll=36.611792,139.835127&spn=.215590,.200260&t=k&hl=ja Utsunomiya Hills Map]

revision.text:
[[File:20070204宇都宮丘陵Tagged.jpg|thumb|right|300px|The right side shows the Utsunomiya Hills.]] '''Utsunomiya Hills''' (宇都宮丘陵) refers to a series of {{ill|hills|ja|丘陵}} located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, stretching northward from the central city area.
== Overview == The Utsunomiya Hills are one of the branching ridges extending in a dendritic pattern from the {{ill|Taishaku Mountain Range|ja|帝釈山脈}} and the {{ill|Ashio Mountains|ja|足尾山地}} toward the {{ill|Kantō Plain|ja|関東平野}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019">{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=2}}</ref> Viewed from the city, the hills begin at {{ill|Myōjin-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|明神山}}, near the [[Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine]], and extend northward through {{ill|Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|八幡山}} to {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山}}. The foothills are flanked by the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tonegawa Basin)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}} to the east and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川}} to the west.
The bedrock of the hills consists of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}, a type of stone quarried as Nagaoka Stone, which is considered a variant of {{ill|Ōya Stone|ja|大谷石}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019"/> The surface is covered by layers of {{ill|loam|ja|ローム (土壌)}}, {{ill|pumice|ja|軽石}}, and other {{ill|ejecta|ja|火山噴出物|volcanic ejecta=volcanic ejecta|lt=volcanic ejecta}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019"/>
== Land Use == Due to its location within the {{ill|city area|ja|市街地}}, the Utsunomiya Hills have been developed for various purposes, including {{ill|shrines|ja|神社}}, {{ill|parks|ja|公園}}, the {{ill|Utsunomiya Velodrome|ja|宇都宮競輪場}}, residential areas, {{ill|schools|ja|学校}}, and cemeteries. It is also an area of historical significance, with numerous archaeological sites from the {{ill|Paleolithic period in Japan|ja|旧石器時代}} onwards. The region contains several {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} clusters, including the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Nagaoka Hundred Caves Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves}}, and {{ill|Kawazuka Kofun Group|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019">{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|pp=2-4}}</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|author=Shibuya Mayuko, Yanagi Makoto, Seichi Ryota|title=Kawazuka Kofun|date=2019-03-29|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/71238|ncid=BB28934995|doi=10.24484/sitereports.71238|series=Utsunomiya City Archaeological Survey Report No. 104|publisher=Utsunomiya City Board of Education|editor=Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ref={{sfnref|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Haguroyama (Tochigi)|ja|羽黒山 (栃木県)}}
== External Links == * [https://www.google.com/maps?ll=36.611792,139.835127&spn=.215590,.200260&t=k&hl=ja Utsunomiya Hills Map]

revision.text:
[[画像:20070204宇都宮丘陵Tagged.jpg|thumb|right|300px|右手が宇都宮丘陵。]] '''宇都宮丘陵'''（うつのみやきゅうりょう）とは[[栃木県]][[宇都宮市]]にあり市街地中心部から北へ伸びる[[丘陵]]である。
== 概要 == [[帝釈山脈]]や[[足尾山地]]から[[関東平野]]に向けて樹枝状に伸びる丘陵の1つである{{sfn|澁谷・梁木・清地|2019|p=2}}。市街地側から見ると、[[明神山 (宇都宮市)|明神山]]の[[宇都宮二荒山神社]]を南端として、[[八幡山 (宇都宮市)|八幡山]]、[[戸祭山]]へと北へ伸びている。丘陵の東西の裾野を[[田川 (利根川水系)|田川]]や[[釜川]]が流れる。
丘陵を構成する基盤は[[凝灰岩]]であり、広義の[[大谷石]]の一種である長岡石が採掘されている{{sfn|澁谷・梁木・清地|2019|p=2}}。表層部は[[ローム (土壌)|ローム]]や[[軽石]]などの[[火山噴出物]]に覆われる{{sfn|澁谷・梁木・清地|2019|p=2}}。
== 土地利用 == [[市街地]]に立地しているため[[神社]]、[[公園]]、[[宇都宮競輪場|競輪場]]、住宅団地、[[学校]]、[[霊園]]等土地開発が進む。[[旧石器時代]]以降の各時代の遺跡が集中する地域でもあり、[[戸祭大塚古墳]]、[[谷口山古墳]]、[[長岡百穴古墳]]、[[瓦塚古墳群]]などの[[古墳群]]がある{{sfn|澁谷・梁木・清地|2019|pp=2-4}}。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|author=澁谷麻友子・梁木誠・清地良太|title=瓦塚古墳|date=2019-03-29|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/71238|ncid=BB28934995|doi=10.24484/sitereports.71238|series=宇都宮市埋蔵文化財調査報告書104|publisher=宇都宮市教育委員会|editor=宇都宮市教育委員会|ref={{sfnref|澁谷・梁木・清地|2019}}}}
== 関連項目 == * [[羽黒山 (栃木県)]]
== 外部リンク == * [https://www.google.com/maps?ll=36.611792,139.835127&spn=.215590,.200260&t=k&hl=ja 宇都宮丘陵マップ]
{{DEFAULTSORT:うつのみやきゆうりよう}} [[Category:日本の丘陵]] [[Category:栃木県の地形]] [[Category:宇都宮市の地理]] [[Category:うつのみや百景]]

revision.text:
[[画像:20070204宇都宮丘陵Tagged.jpg|thumb|right|300px|右手が宇都宮丘陵。]] '''宇都宮丘陵'''（うつのみやきゅうりょう）とは[[栃木県]][[宇都宮市]]にあり市街地中心部から北へ伸びる[[丘陵]]である。
== 概要 == [[帝釈山脈]]や[[足尾山地]]から[[関東平野]]に向けて樹枝状に伸びる丘陵の1つである{{sfn|澁谷・梁木・清地|2019|p=2}}。市街地側から見ると、[[明神山 (宇都宮市)|明神山]]の[[宇都宮二荒山神社]]を南端として、[[八幡山 (宇都宮市)|八幡山]]、[[戸祭山]]へと北へ伸びている。丘陵の東西の裾野を[[田川 (利根川水系)|田川]]や[[釜川]]が流れる。
丘陵を構成する基盤は[[凝灰岩]]であり、広義の[[大谷石]]の一種である長岡石が採掘されている{{sfn|澁谷・梁木・清地|2019|p=2}}。表層部は[[ローム (土壌)|ローム]]や[[軽石]]などの[[火山噴出物]]に覆われる{{sfn|澁谷・梁木・清地|2019|p=2}}。
== 土地利用 == [[市街地]]に立地しているため[[神社]]、[[公園]]、[[宇都宮競輪場|競輪場]]、住宅団地、[[学校]]、[[霊園]]等土地開発が進む。[[旧石器時代]]以降の各時代の遺跡が集中する地域でもあり、[[戸祭大塚古墳]]、[[谷口山古墳]]、[[長岡百穴古墳]]、[[瓦塚古墳群]]などの[[古墳群]]がある{{sfn|澁谷・梁木・清地|2019|pp=2-4}}。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|author=澁谷麻友子・梁木誠・清地良太|title=瓦塚古墳|date=2019-03-29|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/71238|ncid=BB28934995|doi=10.24484/sitereports.71238|series=宇都宮市埋蔵文化財調査報告書104|publisher=宇都宮市教育委員会|editor=宇都宮市教育委員会|ref={{sfnref|澁谷・梁木・清地|2019}}}}
== 関連項目 == * [[羽黒山 (栃木県)]]
== 外部リンク == * [https://www.google.com/maps?ll=36.611792,139.835127&spn=.215590,.200260&t=k&hl=ja 宇都宮丘陵マップ]
{{DEFAULTSORT:うつのみやきゆうりよう}} [[Category:日本の丘陵]] [[Category:栃木県の地形]] [[Category:宇都宮市の地理]] [[Category:うつのみや百景]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:宇都宮丘陵]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11449703]] Wikidata name: Utsunomiya Hills [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:20070204宇都宮丘陵Tagged.jpg|thumb|right|300px|The right side shows the Utsunomiya Hills.]] '''Utsunomiya Hills''' (宇都宮丘陵) refers to a series of [[:en:Hügelland|hills]] located in [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], stretching northward from the central city area.
== Overview == The Utsunomiya Hills are one of the branching ridges extending in a dendritic pattern from the {{ill|Taishaku Mountain Range|ja|帝釈山脈|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishaku Mountain Range|qq=|1=Taishaku Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11481091}} and the {{ill|Ashio Mountains|ja|足尾山地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashio Mountains|qq=|1=Ashio Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11636877}} toward the [[:en:Kantō Plain|Kantō Plain]].<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019">{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=2}}</ref> Viewed from the city, the hills begin at {{ill|Myōjin-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|明神山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-yama (Utsunomiya)|qq=|1=Mount Myōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512575}}, near the [[Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine]], and extend northward through {{ill|Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|八幡山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|qq=|1=Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390953}} to {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri-yama|qq=}}. The foothills are flanked by the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tonegawa Basin)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tonegawa Basin)|qq=|1=Tagawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11576724}} to the east and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamagawa River|qq=|1=Kama River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11648095}} to the west.
The bedrock of the hills consists of [[:en:Tuff|tuff]], a type of stone quarried as Nagaoka Stone, which is considered a variant of [[:en:Ōya stone|Ōya Stone]].<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019"/> The surface is covered by layers of [[:en:Loam|loam]], [[:en:Pumice|pumice]], and other [[:en:Ejecta|volcanic ejecta]].<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019"/>
== Land Use == Due to its location within the {{ill|city area|ja|市街地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/city area|qq=|1=town area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11480697}}, the Utsunomiya Hills have been developed for various purposes, including [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrines]], [[:en:Park|parks]], the [[:en:Utsunomiya Velodrome|Utsunomiya Velodrome]], residential areas, [[:en:School|schools]], and cemeteries. It is also an area of historical significance, with numerous archaeological sites from the [[:en:Paleolithic|Paleolithic period in Japan]] onwards. The region contains several [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] clusters, including the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Nagaoka Hundred Caves Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawazuka Kofun Group|ja|瓦塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawazuka Kofun Group|qq=}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019">{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|pp=2-4}}</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|author=Shibuya Mayuko, Yanagi Makoto, Seichi Ryota|title=Kawazuka Kofun|date=2019-03-29|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/71238|ncid=BB28934995|doi=10.24484/sitereports.71238|series=Utsunomiya City Archaeological Survey Report No. 104|publisher=Utsunomiya City Board of Education|editor=Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ref={{sfnref|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Haguroyama (Tochigi)|ja|羽黒山 (栃木県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguroyama (Tochigi)|qq=|1=Mount Haguro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11610425}}
== External Links == * [https://www.google.com/maps?ll=36.611792,139.835127&spn=.215590,.200260&t=k&hl=ja Utsunomiya Hills Map]
{{translated page|ja|宇都宮丘陵|version=90239413|comment=Imported full ja history}}

The foothills are flanked by the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tonegawa Basin)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tonegawa Basin)|1=Tagawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11576724}} to the east and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamagawa River|1=Kama River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11648095}} to the west.

== Land Use == Due to its location within the {{ill|city area|ja|市街地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/city area|1=town area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11480697}}, the Utsunomiya Hills have been developed for various purposes, including [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrines]], [[:en:Park|parks]], the [[:en:Utsunomiya Velodrome|Utsunomiya Velodrome]], residential areas, [[:en:School|schools]], and cemeteries. It is also an area of historical significance, with numerous archaeological sites from the [[:en:Paleolithic|Paleolithic period in Japan]] onwards. The region contains several [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] clusters, including the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Nagaoka Hundred Caves Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawazuka Kofun Group|ja|瓦塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawazuka Kofun Group|qq=}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019">{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|pp=2-4}}</ref>

== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|author=Shibuya Mayuko, Yanagi Makoto, Seichi Ryota|title=Kawazuka Kofun|date=2019-03-29|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/71238|ncid=BB28934995|doi=10.24484/sitereports.71238|series=Utsunomiya City Archaeological Survey Report No. 104|publisher=Utsunomiya City Board of Education|editor=Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ref={{sfnref|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Haguroyama (Tochigi)|ja|羽黒山 (栃木県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguroyama (Tochigi)|1=Mount Haguro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11610425}}

== Overview == The Utsunomiya Hills are one of the branching ridges extending in a dendritic pattern from the {{ill|Taishaku Mountain Range|ja|帝釈山脈|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishaku Mountain Range|1=Taishaku Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11481091}} and the {{ill|Ashio Mountains|ja|足尾山地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashio Mountains|1=Ashio Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11636877}} toward the [[:en:Kantō Plain|Kantō Plain]].<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019">{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=2}}</ref> Viewed from the city, the hills begin at {{ill|Myōjin-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|明神山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-yama (Utsunomiya)|1=Mount Myōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512575}}, near the [[Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine]], and extend northward through {{ill|Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|八幡山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|1=Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390953}} to {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri-yama|qq=}}. The foothills are flanked by the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tonegawa Basin)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tonegawa Basin)|1=Tagawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11576724}} to the east and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamagawa River|1=Kama River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11648095}} to the west.
The bedrock of the hills consists of [[:en:Tuff|tuff]], a type of stone quarried as Nagaoka Stone, which is considered a variant of [[:en:Ōya stone|Ōya Stone]].<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019"/> The surface is covered by layers of [[:en:Loam|loam]], [[:en:Pumice|pumice]], and other [[:en:Ejecta|volcanic ejecta]].<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019"/>
== Land Use == Due to its location within the {{ill|city area|ja|市街地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/city area|1=town area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11480697}}, the Utsunomiya Hills have been developed for various purposes, including [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrines]], [[:en:Park|parks]], the [[:en:Utsunomiya Velodrome|Utsunomiya Velodrome]], residential areas, [[:en:School|schools]], and cemeteries. It is also an area of historical significance, with numerous archaeological sites from the [[:en:Paleolithic|Paleolithic period in Japan]] onwards. The region contains several [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] clusters, including the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Nagaoka Hundred Caves Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawazuka Kofun Group|ja|瓦塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawazuka Kofun Group|qq=}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019">{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|pp=2-4}}</ref>

== Overview == The Utsunomiya Hills are one of the branching ridges extending in a dendritic pattern from the {{ill|Taishaku Mountain Range|ja|帝釈山脈|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishaku Mountain Range|1=Taishaku Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11481091}} and the {{ill|Ashio Mountains|ja|足尾山地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashio Mountains|1=Ashio Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11636877}} toward the [[:en:Kantō Plain|Kantō Plain]].<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019">{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=2}}</ref> Viewed from the city, the hills begin at {{ill|Myōjin-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|明神山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-yama (Utsunomiya)|1=Mount Myōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512575}}, near the [[Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine]], and extend northward through {{ill|Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|八幡山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|1=Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390953}} to {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri-yama|qq=}}. The foothills are flanked by the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tonegawa Basin)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tonegawa Basin)|1=Tagawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11576724}} to the east and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamagawa River|1=Kama River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11648095}} to the west.
The bedrock of the hills consists of [[:en:Tuff|tuff]], a type of stone quarried as Nagaoka Stone, which is considered a variant of [[:en:Ōya stone|Ōya Stone]].<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019"/> The surface is covered by layers of [[:en:Loam|loam]], [[:en:Pumice|pumice]], and other [[:en:Ejecta|volcanic ejecta]].<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019"/>
== Land Use == Due to its location within the {{ill|city area|ja|市街地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/city area|1=town area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11480697}}, the Utsunomiya Hills have been developed for various purposes, including [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrines]], [[:en:Park|parks]], the [[:en:Utsunomiya Velodrome|Utsunomiya Velodrome]], residential areas, [[:en:School|schools]], and cemeteries. It is also an area of historical significance, with numerous archaeological sites from the [[:en:Paleolithic|Paleolithic period in Japan]] onwards. The region contains several [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] clusters, including the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Nagaoka Hundred Caves Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawazuka Kofun Group|ja|瓦塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawazuka Kofun Group|qq=}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019">{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|pp=2-4}}</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|author=Shibuya Mayuko, Yanagi Makoto, Seichi Ryota|title=Kawazuka Kofun|date=2019-03-29|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/71238|ncid=BB28934995|doi=10.24484/sitereports.71238|series=Utsunomiya City Archaeological Survey Report No. 104|publisher=Utsunomiya City Board of Education|editor=Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ref={{sfnref|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Haguroyama (Tochigi)|ja|羽黒山 (栃木県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguroyama (Tochigi)|1=Mount Haguro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11610425}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:宇都宮丘陵]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11449703]] Wikidata name: Utsunomiya Hills [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:20070204宇都宮丘陵Tagged.jpg|thumb|right|300px|The right side shows the Utsunomiya Hills.]] '''Utsunomiya Hills''' (宇都宮丘陵) refers to a series of [[:en:Hügelland|hills]] located in [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], stretching northward from the central city area.
== Overview == The Utsunomiya Hills are one of the branching ridges extending in a dendritic pattern from the {{ill|Taishaku Mountain Range|ja|帝釈山脈|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishaku Mountain Range|1=Taishaku Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11481091}} and the {{ill|Ashio Mountains|ja|足尾山地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashio Mountains|1=Ashio Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11636877}} toward the [[:en:Kantō Plain|Kantō Plain]].<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019">{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=2}}</ref> Viewed from the city, the hills begin at {{ill|Myōjin-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|明神山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-yama (Utsunomiya)|1=Mount Myōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512575}}, near the [[Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine]], and extend northward through {{ill|Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|八幡山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|1=Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390953}} to {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri-yama|qq=}}. The foothills are flanked by the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tonegawa Basin)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tonegawa Basin)|1=Tagawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11576724}} to the east and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamagawa River|1=Kama River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11648095}} to the west.
The bedrock of the hills consists of [[:en:Tuff|tuff]], a type of stone quarried as Nagaoka Stone, which is considered a variant of [[:en:Ōya stone|Ōya Stone]].<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019"/> The surface is covered by layers of [[:en:Loam|loam]], [[:en:Pumice|pumice]], and other [[:en:Ejecta|volcanic ejecta]].<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019"/>
== Land Use == Due to its location within the {{ill|city area|ja|市街地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/city area|1=town area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11480697}}, the Utsunomiya Hills have been developed for various purposes, including [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrines]], [[:en:Park|parks]], the [[:en:Utsunomiya Velodrome|Utsunomiya Velodrome]], residential areas, [[:en:School|schools]], and cemeteries. It is also an area of historical significance, with numerous archaeological sites from the [[:en:Paleolithic|Paleolithic period in Japan]] onwards. The region contains several [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] clusters, including the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Nagaoka Hundred Caves Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawazuka Kofun Group|ja|瓦塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawazuka Kofun Group|qq=}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019">{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|pp=2-4}}</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|author=Shibuya Mayuko, Yanagi Makoto, Seichi Ryota|title=Kawazuka Kofun|date=2019-03-29|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/71238|ncid=BB28934995|doi=10.24484/sitereports.71238|series=Utsunomiya City Archaeological Survey Report No. 104|publisher=Utsunomiya City Board of Education|editor=Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ref={{sfnref|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Haguroyama (Tochigi)|ja|羽黒山 (栃木県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguroyama (Tochigi)|1=Mount Haguro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11610425}}
== External Links == * [https://www.google.com/maps?ll=36.611792,139.835127&spn=.215590,.200260&t=k&hl=ja Utsunomiya Hills Map]
{{translated page|ja|宇都宮丘陵|version=90239413|comment=Imported full ja history}}

== Overview == The Utsunomiya Hills are one of the branching ridges extending in a dendritic pattern from the {{ill|Taishaku Mountain Range|ja|帝釈山脈|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishaku Mountain Range|1=Taishaku Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11481091}} and the {{ill|Ashio Mountains|ja|足尾山地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashio Mountains|1=Ashio Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11636877}} toward the [[:en:Kantō Plain|Kantō Plain]].<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019">{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=2}}</ref> Viewed from the city, the hills begin at {{ill|Myōjin-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|明神山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-yama (Utsunomiya)|1=Mount Myōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512575}}, near the [[Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine]], and extend northward through {{ill|Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|八幡山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|1=Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390953}} to {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri-yama|qq=}}. The foothills are flanked by the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tonegawa Basin)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tonegawa Basin)|1=Tagawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11576724}} to the east and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamagawa River|1=Kama River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11648095}} to the west.
The bedrock of the hills consists of [[:en:Tuff|tuff]], a type of stone quarried as Nagaoka Stone, which is considered a variant of [[:en:Ōya stone|Ōya Stone]].<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019"/> The surface is covered by layers of [[:en:Loam|loam]], [[:en:Pumice|pumice]], and other [[:en:Ejecta|volcanic ejecta]].<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019"/>
== Land Use == Due to its location within the {{ill|city area|ja|市街地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/city area|1=town area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11480697}}, the Utsunomiya Hills have been developed for various purposes, including [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrines]], [[:en:Park|parks]], the [[:en:Utsunomiya Velodrome|Utsunomiya Velodrome]], residential areas, [[:en:School|schools]], and cemeteries. It is also an area of historical significance, with numerous archaeological sites from the [[:en:Paleolithic|Paleolithic period in Japan]] onwards. The region contains several [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] clusters, including the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Nagaoka Hundred Caves Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawazuka Kofun Group|ja|瓦塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawazuka Kofun Group|qq=}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019">{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|pp=2-4}}</ref>

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:宇都宮丘陵]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11449703]] Wikidata name: Utsunomiya Hills [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:20070204宇都宮丘陵Tagged.jpg|thumb|right|300px|The right side shows the Utsunomiya Hills.]] '''Utsunomiya Hills''' (宇都宮丘陵) refers to a series of [[:en:Hügelland|hills]] located in [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], stretching northward from the central city area.
== Overview == The Utsunomiya Hills are one of the branching ridges extending in a dendritic pattern from the {{ill|Taishaku Mountain Range|ja|帝釈山脈|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishaku Mountain Range|1=Taishaku Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11481091}} and the {{ill|Ashio Mountains|ja|足尾山地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashio Mountains|1=Ashio Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11636877}} toward the [[:en:Kantō Plain|Kantō Plain]].<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019">{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=2}}</ref> Viewed from the city, the hills begin at {{ill|Myōjin-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|明神山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-yama (Utsunomiya)|1=Mount Myōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512575}}, near the [[Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine]], and extend northward through {{ill|Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|八幡山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|1=Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390953}} to {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri-yama|qq=}}. The foothills are flanked by the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tonegawa Basin)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tonegawa Basin)|1=Tagawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11576724}} to the east and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamagawa River|1=Kama River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11648095}} to the west.
The bedrock of the hills consists of [[:en:Tuff|tuff]], a type of stone quarried as Nagaoka Stone, which is considered a variant of [[:en:Ōya stone|Ōya Stone]].<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019"/> The surface is covered by layers of [[:en:Loam|loam]], [[:en:Pumice|pumice]], and other [[:en:Ejecta|volcanic ejecta]].<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019"/>
== Land Use == Due to its location within the {{ill|city area|ja|市街地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/city area|1=town area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11480697}}, the Utsunomiya Hills have been developed for various purposes, including [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrines]], [[:en:Park|parks]], the [[:en:Utsunomiya Velodrome|Utsunomiya Velodrome]], residential areas, [[:en:School|schools]], and cemeteries. It is also an area of historical significance, with numerous archaeological sites from the [[:en:Paleolithic|Paleolithic period in Japan]] onwards. The region contains several [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] clusters, including the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Nagaoka Hundred Caves Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawazuka Kofun Group|ja|瓦塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawazuka Kofun Group|qq=}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019">{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|pp=2-4}}</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|author=Shibuya Mayuko, Yanagi Makoto, Seichi Ryota|title=Kawazuka Kofun|date=2019-03-29|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/71238|ncid=BB28934995|doi=10.24484/sitereports.71238|series=Utsunomiya City Archaeological Survey Report No. 104|publisher=Utsunomiya City Board of Education|editor=Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ref={{sfnref|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Haguroyama (Tochigi)|ja|羽黒山 (栃木県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguroyama (Tochigi)|1=Mount Haguro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11610425}}
== External Links == * [https://www.google.com/maps?ll=36.611792,139.835127&spn=.215590,.200260&t=k&hl=ja Utsunomiya Hills Map]
{{translated page|ja|宇都宮丘陵|version=90239413|comment=Imported full ja history}}

== Land Use == Due to its location within the {{ill|city area|ja|市街地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/city area|1=town area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11480697}}, the Utsunomiya Hills have been developed for various purposes, including [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrines]], [[:en:Park|parks]], the [[:en:Utsunomiya Velodrome|Utsunomiya Velodrome]], residential areas, [[:en:School|schools]], and cemeteries. It is also an area of historical significance, with numerous archaeological sites from the [[:en:Paleolithic|Paleolithic period in Japan]] onwards. The region contains several [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] clusters, including the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Nagaoka Hundred Caves Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawazuka Kofun Group|ja|瓦塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawazuka Kofun Group|qq=}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019">{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|pp=2-4}}</ref>

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:宇都宮丘陵]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:宇都宮丘陵]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:宇都宮丘陵]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11449703]] Wikidata name: Utsunomiya Hills [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:20070204宇都宮丘陵Tagged.jpg|thumb|right|300px|The right side shows the Utsunomiya Hills.]] '''Utsunomiya Hills''' (宇都宮丘陵) refers to a series of [[:en:Hügelland|hills]] located in [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], stretching northward from the central city area.
== Overview == The Utsunomiya Hills are one of the branching ridges extending in a dendritic pattern from the {{ill|Taishaku Mountain Range|ja|帝釈山脈|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishaku Mountain Range|1=Taishaku Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11481091}} and the {{ill|Ashio Mountains|ja|足尾山地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashio Mountains|1=Ashio Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11636877}} toward the [[:en:Kantō Plain|Kantō Plain]].<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019">{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=2}}</ref> Viewed from the city, the hills begin at {{ill|Myōjin-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|明神山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-yama (Utsunomiya)|1=Mount Myōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512575}}, near the [[Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine]], and extend northward through {{ill|Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|八幡山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|1=Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390953}} to {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri-yama|qq=}}. The foothills are flanked by the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tonegawa Basin)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tonegawa Basin)|1=Tagawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11576724}} to the east and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamagawa River|1=Kama River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11648095}} to the west.
The bedrock of the hills consists of [[:en:Tuff|tuff]], a type of stone quarried as Nagaoka Stone, which is considered a variant of [[:en:Ōya stone|Ōya Stone]].<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019"/> The surface is covered by layers of [[:en:Loam|loam]], [[:en:Pumice|pumice]], and other [[:en:Ejecta|volcanic ejecta]].<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019"/>
== Land Use == Due to its location within the {{ill|city area|ja|市街地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/city area|1=town area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11480697}}, the Utsunomiya Hills have been developed for various purposes, including [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrines]], [[:en:Park|parks]], the [[:en:Utsunomiya Velodrome|Utsunomiya Velodrome]], residential areas, [[:en:School|schools]], and cemeteries. It is also an area of historical significance, with numerous archaeological sites from the [[:en:Paleolithic|Paleolithic period in Japan]] onwards. The region contains several [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] clusters, including the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Nagaoka Hundred Caves Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawazuka Kofun Group|ja|瓦塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawazuka Kofun Group|qq=}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019">{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|pp=2-4}}</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|author=Shibuya Mayuko, Yanagi Makoto, Seichi Ryota|title=Kawazuka Kofun|date=2019-03-29|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/71238|ncid=BB28934995|doi=10.24484/sitereports.71238|series=Utsunomiya City Archaeological Survey Report No. 104|publisher=Utsunomiya City Board of Education|editor=Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ref={{sfnref|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Haguroyama (Tochigi)|ja|羽黒山 (栃木県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguroyama (Tochigi)|1=Mount Haguro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11610425}}
== External Links == * [https://www.google.com/maps?ll=36.611792,139.835127&spn=.215590,.200260&t=k&hl=ja Utsunomiya Hills Map]

The foothills are flanked by the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tonegawa Basin)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tonegawa Basin)|1=Tagawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11576724}} to the east and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamagawa River|1=Kama River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11648095}} to the west.
The bedrock of the hills consists of [[:en:Tuff|tuff]], a type of stone quarried as Nagaoka Stone, which is considered a variant of [[:en:Ōya stone|Ōya Stone]].<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019"/> The surface is covered by layers of [[:en:Loam|loam]], [[:en:Pumice|pumice]], and other [[:en:Ejecta|volcanic ejecta]].<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019"/>
== Land Use == Due to its location within the {{ill|city area|ja|市街地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/city area|1=town area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11480697}}, the Utsunomiya Hills have been developed for various purposes, including [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrines]], [[:en:Park|parks]], the [[:en:Utsunomiya Velodrome|Utsunomiya Velodrome]], residential areas, [[:en:School|schools]], and cemeteries. It is also an area of historical significance, with numerous archaeological sites from the [[:en:Paleolithic|Paleolithic period in Japan]] onwards. The region contains several [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] clusters, including the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Nagaoka Hundred Caves Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawazuka Kofun Group|ja|瓦塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawazuka Kofun Group|qq=}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019">{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|pp=2-4}}</ref>
