It is also an area of historical significance, with numerous archaeological sites from the {{ill|Paleolithic|en|Paleolithic|WD=Q40203|lt=Paleolithic period in Japan}} onwards. The region contains several {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} clusters, including the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Nagaoka Hundred Caves Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawazuka Kofun Group|ja|瓦塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawazuka Kofun Group|qq=}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019">{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|pp=2-4}}</ref>

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:宇都宮丘陵]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:宇都宮丘陵]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:宇都宮丘陵]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11449703]] Wikidata name: Utsunomiya Hills [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:20070204宇都宮丘陵Tagged.jpg|thumb|right|300px|The right side shows the Utsunomiya Hills.]] '''Utsunomiya Hills''' (宇都宮丘陵) refers to a series of {{ill|Hügelland|en|Hügelland|WD=Q222912|lt=hills}} located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|en|Utsunomiya|WD=Q200589|lt=Utsunomiya}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}}, stretching northward from the central city area.
== Overview == The Utsunomiya Hills are one of the branching ridges extending in a dendritic pattern from the {{ill|Taishaku Mountain Range|ja|帝釈山脈|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishaku Mountain Range|1=Taishaku Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11481091}} and the {{ill|Ashio Mountains|ja|足尾山地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashio Mountains|1=Ashio Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11636877}} toward the {{ill|Kantō Plain|en|Kantō Plain|WD=Q1124865|lt=Kantō Plain}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019">{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=2}}</ref> Viewed from the city, the hills begin at {{ill|Myōjin-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|明神山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-yama (Utsunomiya)|1=Mount Myōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512575}}, near the [[Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine]], and extend northward through {{ill|Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|八幡山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|1=Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390953}} to {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri-yama|qq=}}. The foothills are flanked by the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tonegawa Basin)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tonegawa Basin)|1=Tagawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11576724}} to the east and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamagawa River|1=Kama River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11648095}} to the west.
The bedrock of the hills consists of {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=tuff}}, a type of stone quarried as Nagaoka Stone, which is considered a variant of {{ill|Ōya stone|en|Ōya stone|WD=Q8081802|lt=Ōya Stone}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019"/> The surface is covered by layers of {{ill|Loam|en|Loam|WD=Q1131303|lt=loam}}, {{ill|Pumice|en|Pumice|WD=Q180044|lt=pumice}}, and other {{ill|Ejecta|en|Ejecta|WD=Q1321876|lt=volcanic ejecta}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019"/>
== Land Use == Due to its location within the {{ill|city area|ja|市街地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/city area|1=town area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11480697}}, the Utsunomiya Hills have been developed for various purposes, including {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrines}}, {{ill|Park|en|Park|WD=Q22698|lt=parks}}, the {{ill|Utsunomiya Velodrome|en|Utsunomiya Velodrome|WD=Q7903284|lt=Utsunomiya Velodrome}}, residential areas, {{ill|School|en|School|WD=Q3914|lt=schools}}, and cemeteries. It is also an area of historical significance, with numerous archaeological sites from the {{ill|Paleolithic|en|Paleolithic|WD=Q40203|lt=Paleolithic period in Japan}} onwards. The region contains several {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} clusters, including the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Nagaoka Hundred Caves Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawazuka Kofun Group|ja|瓦塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawazuka Kofun Group|qq=}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019">{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|pp=2-4}}</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|author=Shibuya Mayuko, Yanagi Makoto, Seichi Ryota|title=Kawazuka Kofun|date=2019-03-29|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/71238|ncid=BB28934995|doi=10.24484/sitereports.71238|series=Utsunomiya City Archaeological Survey Report No. 104|publisher=Utsunomiya City Board of Education|editor=Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ref={{sfnref|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Haguroyama (Tochigi)|ja|羽黒山 (栃木県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguroyama (Tochigi)|1=Mount Haguro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11610425}}
== External Links == * [https://www.google.com/maps?ll=36.611792,139.835127&spn=.215590,.200260&t=k&hl=ja Utsunomiya Hills Map]
{{translated page|ja|宇都宮丘陵|version=90239413|comment=Imported full ja history}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:宇都宮丘陵]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:宇都宮丘陵]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:宇都宮丘陵]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11449703]] Wikidata name: Utsunomiya Hills [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:20070204宇都宮丘陵Tagged.jpg|thumb|right|300px|The right side shows the Utsunomiya Hills.]] '''Utsunomiya Hills''' (宇都宮丘陵) refers to a series of {{ill|Hügelland|en|Hügelland|WD=Q222912|lt=hills}} located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|en|Utsunomiya|WD=Q200589|lt=Utsunomiya}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}}, stretching northward from the central city area.
== Overview == The Utsunomiya Hills are one of the branching ridges extending in a dendritic pattern from the {{ill|Taishaku Mountain Range|ja|帝釈山脈|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishaku Mountain Range|1=Taishaku Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11481091}} and the {{ill|Ashio Mountains|ja|足尾山地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashio Mountains|1=Ashio Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11636877}} toward the {{ill|Kantō Plain|en|Kantō Plain|WD=Q1124865|lt=Kantō Plain}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019">{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=2}}</ref> Viewed from the city, the hills begin at {{ill|Myōjin-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|明神山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-yama (Utsunomiya)|1=Mount Myōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512575}}, near the [[Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine]], and extend northward through {{ill|Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|八幡山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|1=Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390953}} to {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri-yama|qq=}}. The foothills are flanked by the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tonegawa Basin)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tonegawa Basin)|1=Tagawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11576724}} to the east and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamagawa River|1=Kama River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11648095}} to the west.
The bedrock of the hills consists of {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=tuff}}, a type of stone quarried as Nagaoka Stone, which is considered a variant of {{ill|Ōya stone|en|Ōya stone|WD=Q8081802|lt=Ōya Stone}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019"/> The surface is covered by layers of {{ill|Loam|en|Loam|WD=Q1131303|lt=loam}}, {{ill|Pumice|en|Pumice|WD=Q180044|lt=pumice}}, and other {{ill|Ejecta|en|Ejecta|WD=Q1321876|lt=volcanic ejecta}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019"/>
== Land Use == Due to its location within the {{ill|city area|ja|市街地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/city area|1=town area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11480697}}, the Utsunomiya Hills have been developed for various purposes, including {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrines}}, {{ill|Park|en|Park|WD=Q22698|lt=parks}}, the {{ill|Utsunomiya Velodrome|en|Utsunomiya Velodrome|WD=Q7903284|lt=Utsunomiya Velodrome}}, residential areas, {{ill|School|en|School|WD=Q3914|lt=schools}}, and cemeteries. It is also an area of historical significance, with numerous archaeological sites from the {{ill|Paleolithic|en|Paleolithic|WD=Q40203|lt=Paleolithic period in Japan}} onwards. The region contains several {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} clusters, including the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Nagaoka Hundred Caves Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawazuka Kofun Group|ja|瓦塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawazuka Kofun Group|qq=}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019">{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|pp=2-4}}</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|author=Shibuya Mayuko, Yanagi Makoto, Seichi Ryota|title=Kawazuka Kofun|date=2019-03-29|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/71238|ncid=BB28934995|doi=10.24484/sitereports.71238|series=Utsunomiya City Archaeological Survey Report No. 104|publisher=Utsunomiya City Board of Education|editor=Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ref={{sfnref|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Haguroyama (Tochigi)|ja|羽黒山 (栃木県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguroyama (Tochigi)|1=Mount Haguro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11610425}}

== Land Use == Due to its location within the {{ill|city area|ja|市街地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/city area|1=town area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11480697}}, the Utsunomiya Hills have been developed for various purposes, including {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrines}}, {{ill|Park|en|Park|WD=Q22698|lt=parks}}, the {{ill|Utsunomiya Velodrome|en|Utsunomiya Velodrome|WD=Q7903284|lt=Utsunomiya Velodrome}}, residential areas, {{ill|School|en|School|WD=Q3914|lt=schools}}, and cemeteries. The region contains several {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} clusters, including the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Nagaoka Hundred Caves Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawazuka Kofun Group|ja|瓦塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawazuka Kofun Group|qq=}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019">{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|pp=2-4}}</ref>

== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|author=Shibuya Mayuko, Yanagi Makoto, Seichi Ryota|title=Kawazuka Kofun|date=2019-03-29|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/71238|ncid=BB28934995|doi=10.24484/sitereports.71238|series=Utsunomiya City Archaeological Survey Report No. 104|publisher=Utsunomiya City Board of Education|editor=Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ref={{sfnref|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Haguroyama (Tochigi)|ja|羽黒山 (栃木県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguroyama (Tochigi)|1=Mount Haguro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11610425}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:宇都宮丘陵]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:宇都宮丘陵]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:宇都宮丘陵]]
[[File:20070204宇都宮丘陵Tagged.jpg|thumb|right|300px|The right side shows the Utsunomiya Hills.]] '''Utsunomiya Hills''' (宇都宮丘陵) refers to a series of {{ill|Hügelland|en|Hügelland|WD=Q222912|lt=hills|1=Hügelland}} located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|en|Utsunomiya|WD=Q200589|lt=Utsunomiya|1=Utsunomiya}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}, stretching northward from the central city area.
== Overview == The Utsunomiya Hills are one of the branching ridges extending in a dendritic pattern from the {{ill|Taishaku Mountain Range|ja|帝釈山脈|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishaku Mountain Range|1=Taishaku Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11481091}} and the {{ill|Ashio Mountains|ja|足尾山地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashio Mountains|1=Ashio Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11636877}} toward the {{ill|Kantō Plain|en|Kantō Plain|WD=Q1124865|lt=Kantō Plain|1=Kantō Plain}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019">{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=2}}</ref> Viewed from the city, the hills begin at {{ill|Myōjin-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|明神山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-yama (Utsunomiya)|1=Mount Myōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512575}}, near the [[Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine]], and extend northward through {{ill|Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|八幡山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|1=Q11390953|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390953}} to {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri-yama|qq=}}. The foothills are flanked by the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tonegawa Basin)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tonegawa Basin)|1=Tagawa River (Tone River System)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11576724}} to the east and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamagawa River|1=Kama River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11648095}} to the west.
The bedrock of the hills consists of {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=tuff|1=Tuff}}, a type of stone quarried as Nagaoka Stone, which is considered a variant of {{ill|Ōya stone|en|Ōya stone|WD=Q8081802|lt=Ōya Stone|1=Ōya stone}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019"/> The surface is covered by layers of {{ill|Loam|en|Loam|WD=Q1131303|lt=loam|1=Loam}}, {{ill|Pumice|en|Pumice|WD=Q180044|lt=pumice|1=Pumice}}, and other {{ill|Ejecta|en|Ejecta|WD=Q1321876|lt=volcanic ejecta|1=Ejecta}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019"/>
== Land Use == Due to its location within the {{ill|city area|ja|市街地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/city area|1=town area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11480697}}, the Utsunomiya Hills have been developed for various purposes, including {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrines|1=Shinto shrine}}, {{ill|Park|en|Park|WD=Q22698|lt=parks|1=Park}}, the {{ill|Utsunomiya Velodrome|en|Utsunomiya Velodrome|WD=Q7903284|lt=Utsunomiya Velodrome|1=Utsunomiya Velodrome}}, residential areas, {{ill|School|en|School|WD=Q3914|lt=schools|1=School}}, and cemeteries. It is also an area of historical significance, with numerous archaeological sites from the {{ill|Paleolithic|en|Paleolithic|WD=Q40203|lt=Paleolithic period in Japan|1=Paleolithic}} onwards. The region contains several {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} clusters, including the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Nagaoka Hundred Caves Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawazuka Kofun Group|ja|瓦塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawazuka Kofun Group|qq=}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019">{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|pp=2-4}}</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|author=Shibuya Mayuko, Yanagi Makoto, Seichi Ryota|title=Kawazuka Kofun|date=2019-03-29|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/71238|ncid=BB28934995|doi=10.24484/sitereports.71238|series=Utsunomiya City Archaeological Survey Report No. 104|publisher=Utsunomiya City Board of Education|editor=Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ref={{sfnref|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Haguroyama (Tochigi)|ja|羽黒山 (栃木県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguroyama (Tochigi)|1=Mount Haguro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11610425}}

[[File:20070204宇都宮丘陵Tagged.jpg|thumb|right|300px|The right side shows the Utsunomiya Hills.]] '''Utsunomiya Hills''' (宇都宮丘陵) refers to a series of {{ill|Hügelland|en|Hügelland|WD=Q222912|lt=hills|1=Hügelland}} located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|en|Utsunomiya|WD=Q200589|lt=Utsunomiya|1=Utsunomiya}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}, stretching northward from the central city area.
== Overview == The Utsunomiya Hills are one of the branching ridges extending in a dendritic pattern from the {{ill|Taishaku Mountain Range|ja|帝釈山脈|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishaku Mountain Range|1=Taishaku Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11481091}} and the {{ill|Ashio Mountains|ja|足尾山地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashio Mountains|1=Ashio Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11636877}} toward the {{ill|Kantō Plain|en|Kantō Plain|WD=Q1124865|lt=Kantō Plain|1=Kantō Plain}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019">{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=2}}</ref> Viewed from the city, the hills begin at {{ill|Myōjin-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|明神山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-yama (Utsunomiya)|1=Mount Myōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512575}}, near the [[Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine]], and extend northward through {{ill|Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|八幡山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|1=Q11390953|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390953}} to {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri-yama|qq=}}. The foothills are flanked by the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tonegawa Basin)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tonegawa Basin)|1=Tagawa River (Tone River System)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11576724}} to the east and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamagawa River|1=Kama River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11648095}} to the west.
The bedrock of the hills consists of {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=tuff|1=Tuff}}, a type of stone quarried as Nagaoka Stone, which is considered a variant of {{ill|Ōya stone|en|Ōya stone|WD=Q8081802|lt=Ōya Stone|1=Ōya stone}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019"/> The surface is covered by layers of {{ill|Loam|en|Loam|WD=Q1131303|lt=loam|1=Loam}}, {{ill|Pumice|en|Pumice|WD=Q180044|lt=pumice|1=Pumice}}, and other {{ill|Ejecta|en|Ejecta|WD=Q1321876|lt=volcanic ejecta|1=Ejecta}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019"/>
== Land Use == Due to its location within the {{ill|city area|ja|市街地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/city area|1=town area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11480697}}, the Utsunomiya Hills have been developed for various purposes, including {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrines|1=Shinto shrine}}, {{ill|Park|en|Park|WD=Q22698|lt=parks|1=Park}}, the {{ill|Utsunomiya Velodrome|en|Utsunomiya Velodrome|WD=Q7903284|lt=Utsunomiya Velodrome|1=Utsunomiya Velodrome}}, residential areas, {{ill|School|en|School|WD=Q3914|lt=schools|1=School}}, and cemeteries. It is also an area of historical significance, with numerous archaeological sites from the {{ill|Paleolithic|en|Paleolithic|WD=Q40203|lt=Paleolithic period in Japan|1=Paleolithic}} onwards. The region contains several {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} clusters, including the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Nagaoka Hundred Caves Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawazuka Kofun Group|ja|瓦塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawazuka Kofun Group|qq=}}.<ref name="Shibuya et al. 2019">{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|pp=2-4}}</ref>
